title: Weekly Insights Review
summary:
Read here everything you need to know


Things investors need to know
Every time I hear about quantitative easing, the lyrics of the Eagle's 1976 classic " Hotel California " come to mind: ‘You can check out any time you like, but you can never leave'. Quantitative easing is the ultimate tool to pacify markets. Read More

Supramaxes continue to outperform larger bulk carriers
Today we look into how they have fared during the current market downturn and what the main drivers will be in the short and medium term.

children.summary: In the first days of October, freight rates have firmed in the Panamax segment, while Capesize has seen a slight downward trend and Supramax rates have stabilised in the last four weeks.

children.title: The Bulls and the Bears
children.summary: With the global economic picture continuing to be dominated by bearish sentiment, and oil prices falling to lows not seen since the start of the year, the picture couldn't be more different in the tanker market.

children.summary: This was compounded by a rally in energy amid ongoing supply side issues.

children.title: Supply side issues dominated sentiment
children.summary: The spectre of further disruptions saw metals and energy rise. This offset the headwinds from hawkish central banks that helped push the USD higher.
